authority_boundary: Source registry for validator.microcosm.axiom_support_cover authority-scope ordering; it classifies only the authority carried by source bindings plus this read-only validator and does not certify source mutation authority, release authority, public readiness, or anti-axiom rejection.

read_only_validator_projection_authority.meaning: The evaluator can state its own read-only projection boundary, but no obligation witness binding material is present.
read_only_validator_projection_authority.claim_effect: Classifies validator authority only; it cannot witness axiom support.

source_binding_with_read_only_validator_.meaning: The obligation binds source-row material and the evaluator only computes a read-only support projection over it.
source_binding_with_read_only_validator_.claim_effect: Classifies the strongest authority scope currently ordered; it still cannot authorize source mutation, release, public copy, or anti-axiom rejection.

non_laundering_rules: Authority-scope classification is not source mutation authority, release authority, public-readiness authority, or policy approval.
A source binding plus validator readback cannot become proof that an anti-axiom is rejected for a specific obligation.
Generated support-cover output and generated doctrine-lattice projections are never evidence for this registry.
A higher authority-scope class cannot raise an axiom above the meet of positive support and anti-axiom rejection mapping.
unknown_policy: If a computed authority-scope value is outside this source registry, the validator emits unknown_authority_scope_not_in_source_order rather than support.
anti_claims: This registry makes validator authority boundaries computed; it does not grant source mutation or release authority.
This registry does not resolve freshness_state, domain_scope, or projection_scope.
This registry does not verify exact_obligation_rejection or subsumes_obligation mapping.
